title: Sorbus pohuashanensis
taxonomy: Sorbus pohuashanensis is classified in the family Rosaceae, which includes other rose family plants such as apples and pears. This classification is based on the plant's shared characteristics with other members of the Rosaceae family. More specifically, Sorbus pohuashanensis is a species of rowan, a type of flowering plant commonly found in mountainous regions. The genus Sorbus, to which Sorbus pohuashanensis belongs, is native to the Northern Hemisphere and includes a variety of rowan species. Sorbus pohuashanensis is a unique species within this genus, characterized by its specific growth habits and physical characteristics.
uses: Sorbus pohuashanensis is a plant species native to Taiwan. Although information on this plant is limited, research suggests that the plant has medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and fruits are used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever and inflammation. The exact mechanisms and effectiveness of its medicinal uses require further study. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal applications.

preparations: Sorbus pohuashanensis is a species of plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
